description: Letter to a certain Abū Saʿd ('the dear boy') likely from his father. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: ca. 12th century (paleographic dating). Contains inter alia a moralizing rebuke for having sent lashon ha-raʿ, followed by a detailed order for an ʿarḍī cloth for the sabbath and holidays worth 60–70 dirhams. There are 2-3 lines in a different hand appended after the end of the letter. (Information in part from Goitein's index card.)
description_he: מכתב לאבו סעד, כנראה מאביו, עם תוכחה מוסרית והזמנת בד
